sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root נָפַל to fall).-

(1) what falls off; Amo 8:6, מַפַּל בַּר “what falls off from corn,” husk. Hence-

(2) something pendulous, loose; (the Roman poets also used cadere of things which hung loosely; see Gronov. ad Stat. Sylv. 38); Job 41:15, מַפְּלֵי בָשָׂר “the pendulous parts of his flesh,” on the belly of the crocodile, flabby parts (die Wammen).
